name: MetLife Stadium
description: MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey is the crown jewel of the 2026 FIFA World Cup. As the venue for the Final on July 19, 2026, it will host the most-watched sporting event in history. Home to both the New York Giants and Jets of the NFL, this 82,500-seat colosseum has hosted Super Bowls and WrestleMania events. The stadium features a state-of-the-art open-air design with excellent sightlines from every seat.

name: SoFi Stadium
description: SoFi Stadium in Inglewood, California is one of the most technologically advanced stadiums in the world. Its stunning translucent roof and 70,000-seat capacity make it a perfect World Cup venue. Home to the LA Rams and Chargers, SoFi also hosted Super Bowl LVI. Los Angeles brings Hollywood glamour to the beautiful game.

name: AT&T Stadium
description: AT&T Stadium in Arlington, Texas is an engineering marvel featuring the world's largest column-free interior and a retractable roof. Home to the Dallas Cowboys, this 80,000-seat venue features the iconic center-hung video display. Texas's passionate sports culture guarantees an electric atmosphere.

name: Levi's Stadium
description: Levi's Stadium in Santa Clara brings Silicon Valley innovation to the World Cup. The 68,500-capacity home of the San Francisco 49ers features cutting-edge technology and sustainable design, making it one of the most modern stadiums in North America.

name: Hard Rock Stadium
description: Hard Rock Stadium in Miami Gardens is a tropical paradise for football fans. Recently renovated with a stunning canopy roof, this 65,326-seat venue hosts the Miami Dolphins, the Miami Open tennis tournament, and Formula 1. Miami's vibrant Latin culture adds a unique flavor.

name: Mercedes-Benz Stadium
description: Mercedes-Benz Stadium in Atlanta features one of the world's most iconic retractable roofs — an eight-panel design inspired by the Roman Pantheon. The 71,000-seat venue is home to Atlanta United FC (MLS) and the Atlanta Falcons (NFL).

name: Lumen Field
description: Lumen Field in Seattle is renowned as one of the loudest stadiums in the world. Home to the Seattle Sounders FC and Seattle Seahawks, the 69,000-capacity venue's open-end design amplifies crowd noise to legendary levels.

name: NRG Stadium
description: NRG Stadium in Houston features a retractable roof, making it ideal for summer World Cup matches in the Texas heat. The 72,220-seat venue has hosted Super Bowls and is home to the Houston Texans.

name: Lincoln Financial Field
description: Lincoln Financial Field in Philadelphia is famous for its passionate fans. The 69,796-seat home of the Philadelphia Eagles sits in the city where American independence was born, adding historical significance to World Cup matches.

name: Arrowhead Stadium
description: Arrowhead Stadium in Kansas City holds the Guinness World Record for the loudest crowd roar at a sports event. The 76,416-seat home of the Kansas City Chiefs promises an unforgettable atmosphere for World Cup matches.

name: Gillette Stadium
description: Gillette Stadium in Foxborough serves the Boston metropolitan area. Home to the New England Patriots and New England Revolution, the 65,878-seat venue brings New England's rich sporting heritage to the World Cup.

name: Estadio Azteca
description: Estadio Azteca is the only stadium in the world to have hosted two FIFA World Cup Finals (1970 and 1986). This legendary 87,523-seat venue in Mexico City sits at 2,200 meters altitude, creating unique playing conditions. It witnessed Maradona's 'Hand of God' and 'Goal of the Century' in 1986. In 2026, it makes history again as the first venue to host three World Cups.

name: Estadio Akron
description: Estadio Akron in Guadalajara is home to CD Guadalajara (Chivas), one of Mexico's most beloved clubs. The 49,850-capacity stadium features striking volcanic rock-inspired architecture that reflects Mexico's rich cultural heritage.

name: Estadio BBVA
description: Estadio BBVA in Monterrey is one of the most modern stadiums in Latin America. Home to CF Monterrey, the 53,500-seat venue features dramatic views of the Sierra Madre mountains, creating one of the most picturesque settings in world football.

name: BMO Field
description: BMO Field in Toronto is the home of Toronto FC and Canada's national teams. Expanded to 45,736 seats for the World Cup, this venue sits on the Lake Ontario waterfront. Toronto's multiculturalism ensures that every team will feel fan support.

name: BC Place
description: BC Place in Vancouver features a retractable roof and stunning views of the North Shore Mountains. The 54,500-seat venue has hosted Olympic ceremonies (2010 Winter Olympics) and is home to the Vancouver Whitecaps FC. Vancouver's Pacific Rim location makes it the gateway for Asian fans.
